pairs.question: What is the Colfax Spring City Commercial Historic District and when was it recognized?
pairs.answer: The Colfax Spring City Commercial Historic District is a nationally recognized historic district located in Colfax, Iowa, United States. It was listed on the National Register of Historic Places in 2018.

pairs.question: How many resources were included in the Colfax Spring City Commercial Historic District at the time of its nomination?
pairs.answer: At the time of its nomination, the Colfax Spring City Commercial Historic District contained 48 resources, which included 28 contributing buildings, two contributing sites, two contributing structures, 12 non-contributing buildings, and four non-contributing structures.

pairs.question: What were the key factors that led to the development of Colfax, Iowa, as a town in the mid-19th century?
pairs.answer: Colfax was platted in 1866 or 1867 as a railroad and farm-to-market town along the Chicago, Rock Island and Pacific Railroad. The discovery of mineral water in 1875 further spurred growth by establishing a mineral water-based health industry in the area.

pairs.question: What types of buildings and structures are found within the Colfax Spring City Commercial Historic District?
pairs.answer: The historic district includes civic buildings, churches, retail establishments, fraternal organizations, banks, automobile-related structures, and two parks. Most buildings range from one to three stories, with masonry construction being the most common. The former Presbyterian Church parsonage is the only house in the district and the only first-generation building remaining.

pairs.question: What are some of the architectural styles represented in the Colfax Spring City Commercial Historic District?
pairs.answer: The architectural styles in the district include Italianate, Neoclassical, Tudor, Late Gothic Revival, Mediterranean Revival, and Modern.

pairs.question: Why are there limited first-generation buildings in the Colfax Spring City Commercial Historic District?
pairs.answer: Many of the buildings from the 1860s-1890s era were destroyed by fires or tornadoes, leaving only the former Presbyterian Church parsonage, the sole first-generation building still standing in the district.

pairs.question: How did the economy of Colfax, Iowa, evolve after the decline of the mineral water industry?
pairs.answer: After the mineral water-based health industry declined, Colfax expanded its farm-to-market economy during the late inter-war and post-World War II years.
